inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlinks no longer dereferenced — behavioral regression from old copyDir — The old copyDir used fs.statSync (follows symlinks) and fs.copyFileSync (dereferences sources), producing a self-contained build output. The replacement fs.cpSync defaults to dereference: false, preserving symlinks as-is. This risks dangling symlinks in dist/ for symlinks pointing outside public/, and cpSync will throw for symlinks whose targets reside outside the source tree (when verbatimSymlinks defaults to false).

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlinks no longer dereferenced in template scaffolding copy — Same dereference issue as prepareOutDir: the old copy function used statSync/copyFileSync which resolved symlinks, but the new fs.cpSync call preserves them. Impact is lower here since templates rarely contain symlinks, but the behavioral change is real.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Lost self-copy guard when outDir is nested inside publicDir — The old copyDir skipped entries where srcFile === destDir, preventing infinite-copy when outDir is a child of publicDir (e.g., public/dist). fs.cpSync throws when src === dest exactly but does NOT guard the nested case where dest is a subdirectory discovered during the recursive walk, so this supported but unusual configuration would produce incorrect output or an error.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

Two behavioral regressions found in the switch from custom copyDir to fs.cpSync: (1) symlinks are no longer dereferenced, risking dangling symlinks or errors in the build output, and (2) the self-copy guard when outDir is nested inside publicDir is lost, which could cause incorrect output or errors for that supported configuration.

**3** finding(s): 🔴 0  ·  🟡 3  ·  🔵 0

- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlinks no longer dereferenced — behavioral regression from old copyDir — The old copyDir used fs.statSync (follows symlinks) and fs.copyFileSync (dereferences sources), producing a self-contained build output. The replacement fs.cpSync defaults to dereference: false, preserving symlinks as-is. This risks dangling symlinks in dist/ for symlinks pointing outside public/, and cpSync will throw for symlinks whose targets reside outside the source tree (when verbatimSymlinks defaults to false).

  ```suggestion
  fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, dereference: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })
  ```
- 🟡 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlinks no longer dereferenced in template scaffolding copy — Same dereference issue as prepareOutDir: the old copy function used statSync/copyFileSync which resolved symlinks, but the new fs.cpSync call preserves them. Impact is lower here since templates rarely contain symlinks, but the behavioral change is real.
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Lost self-copy guard when outDir is nested inside publicDir — The old copyDir skipped entries where srcFile === destDir, preventing infinite-copy when outDir is a child of publicDir (e.g., public/dist). fs.cpSync throws when src === dest exactly but does NOT guard the nested case where dest is a subdirectory discovered during the recursive walk, so this supported but unusual configuration would produce incorrect output or an error.

---
